channel.title: fat16 on IT notes
channel.description: Recent content in fat16 on IT notes

item.title: Fat16
item.description: When trying to update a bios from an USB pen, probably you will need FAT16 or FAT32, to do this from macOS try: diskutil partitiondisk /dev/disk3 1 MBR "MS-DOS FAT16" "NONAME" 0B /dev/disk3 - disk device to be partitioned, found with ‘diskutil list’ 1 - number of partitions, optional, but if given must match MBR - partition Scheme (eg, DOS=MBR, Mac=GPT) MS-DOS FAT16 - partition type (or fat32) * NONAME - partition name 0B - partition size (0B=maximum) Then:
